claim: KeHE's Administrative Allowance Program (AAP) is a 2% off-invoice allowance for brands in their first year that opts them out of intro allowances, MCB fees, and manual chargeback charges
speaker: Tim Dornfeld
easyops_relevance: Direct validation of EasyOps's target use case — new brands face 'cost uncertainty' from unexpected fees on first POs
gtm_use: Content/outreach: 'Here's what KeHE's own VP says about the fee shock new brands face'
quote: One of the most challenging things for new and emerging brands is that cost uncertainty — getting hit with those first POs and you're expecting this much to come in but then these new fees hit you and all of a sudden you're scrambling for cash flow

claim: Regional distributors don't charge chargebacks or slotting fees — only UNFI and KeHE do at scale
speaker: Vanessa White
episode: #136: Growing Jaju Pierogi with Social Media Pro Vanessa White
easyops_relevance: Validates the ICP pain: chargebacks/slotting only become a problem when brands move from regional to national distribution
gtm_use: Content: 'The hidden cost of scaling from regional to national distribution'
quote: We got a mistake chargeback from UNFI for $15,000 at Christmas time — an erroneous slotting fee that wasn't meant to happen

claim: The only margin that matters is contribution margin, not product margin — sophisticated investors see through high product margin
speaker: Alex Michaelsen
episode: #237 - Inside Founders & Funders with Alex Michaelsen
easyops_relevance: Frames EasyOps as a contribution margin improvement tool, not just a cost recovery tool
gtm_use: Investor pitch: 'We improve the metric that determines whether you can raise your next round'
quote: When you're first getting started, everyone focuses on product margin because it's the highest number. But the more sophisticated the investors you work with, the more they'll see through that. There's only one margin that matters and it's contribution margin

claim: Payment terms of net 30/60/90 create cash flow crises for emerging brands that can't float inventory costs while waiting for retailer payment
speaker: Gloria Allorbi
episode: Founder Feature: Gloria Allorbi of Gloria's Shito
easyops_relevance: Deduction errors compound the cash flow problem — if you're waiting 90 days for payment AND losing 5-15% to incorrect deductions, the math breaks
gtm_use: Content: 'Net 60 payment terms + erroneous deductions = why your runway is shorter than you think'
quote: When retailers put a purchase order, there are payment terms of net 30, net 60, net 90 — meaning I'm not getting that money upfront. So I have to be able to afford that inventory with capital to then wait for the retailer to pay me

claim: KeHE MCB fees and performance fees on manual chargebacks are among the hidden costs that hit new brands unexpectedly
speaker: Tim Dornfeld
easyops_relevance: MCB fees are exactly what EasyOps's rule-reviewer audits for
gtm_use: Outreach: Reference this episode when reaching out to brands that recently joined KeHE
quote: Intro allowances when going into a DC, MCB fees, extra performance fees on manual chargebacks or charges coming back from retailers

claim: Brands need a diversified distribution strategy — regional distributors as buffer against UNFI/KeHE chargeback volatility
speaker: Vanessa White
episode: #136: Growing Jaju Pierogi with Social Media Pro Vanessa White
easyops_relevance: Even with diversification, brands still sell through UNFI/KeHE for chain grocery — they can't avoid the deduction problem
gtm_use: Content: 'Diversification protects your top line, but who's protecting your bottom line from deductions?'
quote: If something happens — we got a mistake chargeback for $15,000 — at least we have all these other distributors that are going to be paying their bills with no surprises

claim: Founders don't understand the different types of margins until years into running their business
speaker: Alex Michaelsen
episode: #237 - Inside Founders & Funders with Alex Michaelsen
easyops_relevance: Education opportunity — EasyOps can be the brand that teaches margin literacy alongside deduction recovery
gtm_use: Content pillar: 'CPG Margin Literacy' series on Jordan's LinkedIn
quote: It took me a couple years to kind of figure out what the hell everyone was talking about on margin. And oftentimes when you hear it on podcasts or LinkedIn, it's just not everyone is saying a contradictory definition

claim: Trade spend and promotional funding is a significant budget line item that emerging brands underestimate
speaker: Multiple guests across episodes
easyops_relevance: Trade spend visibility is EasyOps's core value proposition — brands can't manage what they can't measure
gtm_use: Audit tool positioning: 'See where your trade spend actually goes'
